activities.instructions: Choose the form that describes a past state which is no longer true.
activities.prompt: This building ___ a post office, but now it is a café.
activities.explanation: Un estado anterior que ya no continúa se expresa con «used to be».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct form for a discontinued habit.
activities.prompt: I ___ to school, but now I take the bus.
activities.explanation: «Used to cycle» contrasta el hábito anterior con el presente.

activities.instructions: Choose the correctly formed question.
activities.prompt: Ask about walking to school in the past.

options.text: Did you use to walk to school?
Did you used to walk to school?
Were you use to walk to school?
Did you use to walked to school?

activities.explanation: Después de «did» usamos «use to + infinitivo».

activities.instructions: Choose the correctly formed negative sentence.
activities.prompt: Describe a food you did not enjoy in the past.

options.text: I didn't use to like olives.
I didn't used to like olives.
I not used to liked olives.
I wasn't use to like olives.

activities.explanation: La negativa editorial es «didn't use to + infinitivo».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct verb form after «would».
activities.prompt: When we were children, we would ___ outside until dark.
activities.explanation: Después de «would» usamos el infinitivo sin «to»: «play».

activities.instructions: Choose the form that correctly describes past possession.
activities.prompt: My uncle ___ an old sailing boat.
activities.explanation: La posesión es un estado y usa «used to have».

activities.instructions: Choose the form for a repeated action inside a clear past frame.
activities.prompt: Whenever we stayed with our grandparents, we ___ in the garden after breakfast.
activities.explanation: El marco está establecido y «would help» describe la acción repetida.

activities.instructions: Choose the clearest standalone description of a discontinued habit.
activities.prompt: No past time frame has been given yet.

options.text: I used to visit the market every Saturday.
I would visit the market every Saturday.
I use to visited the market every Saturday.
I would to visit the market every Saturday.

activities.explanation: Sin marco previo, «used to» expresa de forma inequívoca el hábito pasado.

activities.instructions: Choose the natural form for a completed period with explicit frequency.
activities.prompt: While I was abroad in 2019, I ___ my parents every Sunday.
activities.explanation: El past simple expresa naturalmente un hábito dentro de un periodo cerrado.

activities.instructions: Choose the form meaning that the speaker had an old habit.
activities.prompt: Before changing jobs, I ___ at five every morning.
activities.explanation: «Used to get up» describe el hábito anterior; no significa estar acostumbrado.

activities.instructions: Choose the form meaning «I am accustomed to it now».
activities.prompt: Early starts are normal for me now; I ___ before six.
activities.explanation: «Be used to» significa estar acostumbrado y va seguido de `-ing`.

activities.instructions: Choose the form meaning that adaptation happened.
activities.prompt: The traffic bothered me at first, but eventually I ___ it.
activities.explanation: «Got used to» indica que la persona se acostumbró.

activities.instructions: Choose the form for a repeated bedtime action.
activities.prompt: When we stayed with her, my grandmother ___ us a story before bed.
activities.explanation: El marco pasado está claro y la acción repetida usa «would tell».

activities.instructions: Choose the form for a past state of knowledge.
activities.prompt: My grandmother ___ dozens of traditional songs.
activities.explanation: «Know» expresa un estado y se combina con «used to».

activities.instructions: Choose the interpretation of «wouldn't» that fits the context.
activities.prompt: The old printer wouldn't start, however many times we pressed the button.

options.text: It failed or refused to start.
It had no regular hobby of starting.
It was not accustomed to starting.
It did not use to be a printer.

activities.explanation: Aquí «wouldn't» expresa fallo persistente o rechazo, no un hábito neutral.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct form for a current habit.
activities.prompt: This describes the present, not the past: I ___ to work.
activities.explanation: `Used to` no tiene presente; el hábito actual usa present simple.

activities.instructions: Choose the form for a place or thing that no longer exists.
activities.prompt: There ___ a bookshop on this corner, but it was demolished.
activities.explanation: La existencia anterior es un estado: «There used to be».

activities.instructions: Choose the form for a repeated dynamic event in a clear frame.
activities.prompt: On cold mornings, the old engine ___ rough for the first minute.
activities.explanation: Aquí «sound» describe un evento repetido en cada mañana fría.

activities.instructions: Choose the statement that accurately compares the two forms.
activities.prompt: When I was a child, I ___ my aunt every Friday.

options.text: can use either “used to visit” or “would visit”

activities.explanation: Con una acción repetida y marco claro, ambas formas son posibles.

activities.instructions: Choose the correctly formed question about a past state.
activities.prompt: Ask whether a neighbourhood was quieter in the past.

options.text: Did this area use to be quieter?
Did this area used to be quieter?
Would this area be quieter as a past state?
Was this area use to be quieter?

activities.explanation: La pregunta editorial usa «Did ... use to be ...?».

activities.instructions: Choose the correctly formed negative about a former state.
activities.prompt: The speaker was not afraid of dogs in the past.

options.text: I didn't use to be afraid of dogs.
I didn't used to be afraid of dogs.
I wouldn't be afraid of dogs.
I wasn't use to be afraid of dogs.

activities.explanation: La negativa de un estado anterior usa «didn't use to be».

activities.instructions: Choose the form that cannot be confused with a conditional meaning.
activities.prompt: No previous sentence establishes a past period; state a former habit clearly.

options.text: We used to walk along the harbour after dinner.
We would walk along the harbour after dinner.
We would walked along the harbour after dinner.
We used to walking along the harbour after dinner.

activities.explanation: Sin marco previo, «used to» comunica inequívocamente el hábito anterior.

activities.instructions: Choose the forms for repeated actions in an established routine.
activities.prompt: On Sundays at our grandparents' house, Dad ___ and we ___ afterwards.

options.text: used to cooking / would to wash up
was used to cook / used wash up

activities.explanation: Las dos acciones repetidas usan «would + infinitivo» dentro del marco dominical.

activities.instructions: Choose the forms that distinguish a state from a repeated action.
activities.prompt: When Lena lived in the village, she ___ everyone and she ___ at each neighbour's door before the festival.

options.text: would knew / did use to called

activities.explanation: El conocimiento es un estado; llamar era una acción repetida en el marco indicado.

activities.instructions: Choose the forms matching a former habit and later adaptation.
activities.prompt: I ___ paper maps, but after a month I ___ the navigation app.

options.text: used to rely on / got used to using
was used to rely on / used to use
used to relying on / got used to use
would relied on / got use to using

activities.explanation: El hábito anterior usa «used to rely»; la adaptación usa «got used to using».
